bug_data.summary: Enable NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution for video overlay on Windows only during power charge
bug_data.description:
"NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution for video overlay" support was added by  Bug 1823135. But it is not enabled by default. Since enabling it consumes more power.

Chromium enables it only when power is charging.
- https://source.chromium.org/chromium/chromium/src/+/main:ui/gl/swap_chain_presenter.cc;l=1891;drc=c78381133ae4a3c49b418dda23efe4f974227cd7

Then it seems better to enable it only when PC is power charged.

crashes.signature: EMPTY: no frame data available; EmptyMinidump

bug_analysis.root_cause: NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution for video overlay is not enabled by default because enabling it consumes more power, and it should only be enabled when the PC is charging to optimize power consumption.
symptoms: NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution is disabled by default despite support being added
Potential increased power consumption if enabled when not charging
reproduction_steps: Use Firefox on Windows with NVIDIA RTX GPU
Observe that NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution is not enabled by default when on battery power
Plug in power and observe that the feature becomes enabled
bug_analysis.technical_details: The feature was added in Bug 1823135 but disabled by default due to power consumption concerns. Chromium enables this feature only when the device is charging, as seen in the referenced Chromium source code. Gecko supports power status notifications but does not notify the GPU process currently. The patch enables NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution only during power charge on Windows, targeting NVIDIA GPUs with driver version 530+ and Intel Gen10+ GPUs. The relevant code changes are pushed to Mozilla's autoland repository.
bug_analysis.user_impact: Users benefit from enhanced video quality via NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution when their device is plugged in, avoiding excessive battery drain when on battery power.

common_patterns: Crashes related to memory allocation failures during garbage collection
OOM (Out Of Memory) conditions triggered by failure to allocate new chunks in the JS nursery
Crashes occurring under specific GPU/video overlay scenarios on Windows during power charging state
potential_root_causes: Insufficient memory management or leaks causing memory exhaustion in the JS nursery
Interaction issues between NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution overlay and Firefox's memory usage patterns
Platform-specific (Windows) power state influencing GPU/video overlay behavior leading to unstable memory allocation
relevant_fix_clues: Bug 1851736 aims to enable NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution only during power charge states, indicating a fix by restricting feature activation to specific power conditions
OOM crash in nursery GC suggests that fixes may involve improving memory allocation strategies or limiting resource-intensive features under constrained memory scenarios
similar_bugs_context.summary: These similar bugs suggest that crashes occur due to memory allocation failures during garbage collection in scenarios involving NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution video overlays on Windows. The issues seem tied to how memory is managed when the system is under certain power conditions. Restricting the feature's activation to power charge states and improving memory handling during GC are likely effective mitigations, pointing to a root cause involving resource exhaustion triggered by GPU overlay interactions under specific system states.

similar_bugs_analysis.summary: These similar bugs suggest that crashes occur due to memory allocation failures during garbage collection in scenarios involving NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution video overlays on Windows. The issues seem tied to how memory is managed when the system is under certain power conditions. Restricting the feature's activation to power charge states and improving memory handling during GC are likely effective mitigations, pointing to a root cause involving resource exhaustion triggered by GPU overlay interactions under specific system states.

refined_analysis.root_cause: NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution is disabled on battery power to save energy but should enable automatically when charging.
symptoms: Video Super Resolution remains off on battery despite hardware support.
Users only get enhanced video quality when device is charging.
reproduction_steps: Run Firefox on Windows with NVIDIA RTX GPU (driver 530+).
Verify Video Super Resolution is disabled on battery power.
Plug in charger and verify feature enables automatically.
refined_analysis.technical_details: The feature introduced in Bug 1823135 disables Video Super Resolution on battery. Chromium enables it only when charging by listening to power status events. Gecko's dom/battery handles battery events but does not forward charging state changes to the GPU process. The fix adds forwarding of power state changes from hal/HalBatteryInformation.cpp through dom/battery/BatteryManager.cpp to gpu/GPUProcessHost.cpp. gfx/VideoOverlay.cpp enables Video Super Resolution only when charging. The feature is limited to NVIDIA GPUs (driver 530+) and Intel Gen10+ GPUs on Windows.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Users get improved video quality when charging without increased battery drain on battery power.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: Implement forwarding of charging state changes from hal/HalBatteryInformation.cpp through dom/battery/BatteryManager.cpp to gpu/GPUProcessHost.cpp. Modify gfx/VideoOverlay.cpp to enable NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution only when charging. Restrict activation to NVIDIA GPUs with driver 530+ and Intel Gen10+ GPUs on Windows.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: On Windows, forward charging state from hal/HalBatteryInformation.cpp to dom/battery/BatteryManager.cpp and then to gpu/GPUProcessHost.cpp. Update gfx/VideoOverlay.cpp to enable NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution only when charging. Targets NVIDIA GPUs (driver 530+) and Intel Gen10+ GPUs.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The analysis covers root cause, symptoms, and affected components well but is overly verbose and mixes background with implementation details, reducing focus. It lacks explicit function names or precise code paths, which lowers AI-readiness. The reproduction steps and files are clear and relevant. The solution sketch is high-level and could be more actionable by naming key functions or data flows. The optimized_context is too long and not sufficiently concise or focused on exact file responsibilities.

filtered_context.concise_summary: On Windows, forward charging state changes from hal/HalBatteryInformation.cpp through dom/battery/BatteryManager.cpp to gpu/GPUProcessHost.cpp. Modify gfx/VideoOverlay.cpp to enable NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution only when charging. Restrict activation to NVIDIA GPUs with driver 530+ and Intel Gen10+ GPUs.
core_symptoms: Video Super Resolution remains disabled on battery power despite hardware support.
technical_constraints: Enable Video Super Resolution only when charging
Target NVIDIA GPUs with driver 530+ and Intel Gen10+ GPUs
